title: The Plant on My Desk
text_zh: 半年前同事送了我一盆小绿植，说放在办公桌上可以缓解眼睛疲劳。我收下了，但其实并没有太在意。浇水常常忘记，有一次出差一个星期回来发现叶子都蔫了。我赶紧浇了水，第二天它居然又恢复了精神。从那以后我开始认真照顾它，每天早上到办公室的第一件事就是看看它需不需要浇水。慢慢地我发现它长出了新叶子，颜色比旧叶子浅一些，嫩嫩的。有一天阳光特别好，我把它搬到窗台上晒太阳，下午的时候它的叶子好像比早上更绿了。照顾一棵植物的过程教会了我耐心。它不会因为你着急就长得快一点，也不会因为你忽略它就马上死掉。它有自己的节奏，你只需要持续地给它需要的东西。也许对待自己的成长，也应该像对待一棵植物一样有耐心。
text_pinyin: Bàn nián qián tóngshì sòng le wǒ yì pén xiǎo lǜzhí, shuō fàng zài bàngōngzhuō shàng kěyǐ huǎnjiě yǎnjīng píláo. Wǒ shōu xià le, dàn qíshí bìng méiyǒu tài zàiyì. Jiāo shuǐ chángcháng wàngjì, yǒu yí cì chūchāi yí gè xīngqī huílái fāxiàn yèzi dōu niān le. Wǒ gǎnjǐn jiāo le shuǐ, dì èr tiān tā jūrán yòu huīfù le jīngshén. Cóng nà yǐhòu wǒ kāishǐ rènzhēn zhàogù tā, měi tiān zǎoshang dào bàngōngshì de dì yī jiàn shì jiù shì kàn kàn tā xū bù xūyào jiāo shuǐ. Mànmàn de wǒ fāxiàn tā zhǎng chū le xīn yèzi, yánsè bǐ jiù yèzi qiǎn yìxiē, nèn nèn de. Yǒu yì tiān yángguāng tèbié hǎo, wǒ bǎ tā bān dào chuāngtái shàng shài tàiyáng, xiàwǔ de shíhou tā de yèzi hǎoxiàng bǐ zǎoshang gèng lǜ le. Zhàogù yì kē zhíwù de guòchéng jiāo huì le wǒ nàixīn. Tā bú huì yīnwèi nǐ zháojí jiù zhǎng de kuài yìdiǎn, yě bú huì yīnwèi nǐ hūlüè tā jiù mǎshàng sǐ diào. Tā yǒu zìjǐ de jiézòu, nǐ zhǐ xūyào chíxù de gěi tā xūyào de dōngxi. Yěxǔ duìdài zìjǐ de chéngzhǎng, yě yīnggāi xiàng duìdài yì kē zhíwù yíyàng yǒu nàixīn.
text_en: Six months ago a colleague gave me a small plant, saying putting it on my desk would ease eye strain. I accepted it but didn't really care. I often forgot to water it, and once after a week-long business trip I returned to find its leaves all wilted. I quickly watered it, and the next day it had surprisingly perked back up. After that I started caring for it properly — the first thing each morning at the office was checking if it needed water. Gradually I noticed it grew new leaves, slightly lighter than the old ones, soft and tender. One day when the sun was especially good, I moved it to the windowsill to bask, and by afternoon its leaves seemed greener than in the morning. The process of caring for a plant taught me patience. It won't grow faster because you're anxious, and it won't die immediately because you neglect it. It has its own rhythm; you just need to consistently give it what it needs. Maybe we should approach our own growth with the same patience as caring for a plant.

questions.q_zh: 出差回来以后绿植怎么了？
questions.q_en: What happened to the plant after the business trip?

options.pinyin: zhǎng le hěn duō xīn yèzi

options.text_en: Was cared for by a colleague

questions.q_zh: 照顾植物教会了作者什么？
questions.q_en: What did caring for the plant teach the author?

title: A Quiet Sunday
text_zh: 周日早上醒来已经九点了，窗帘缝里透进来一条阳光。我没有马上起床，躺在被子里听外面的声音。楼下有人在遛狗，远处有小孩在笑，偶尔传来一声鸟叫。今天没有任何安排，这种感觉很久没有过了。起床以后我煮了咖啡，把早餐摆在阳台的小桌上，一边吃一边看楼下的人来人往。下午我翻了一本放了很久没看完的书，看了大概五十页。有时候看着看着走神了，就放下书发一会儿呆，然后再接着看。傍晚的时候我去楼下散了一圈步，买了一个西瓜回来。晚上看了一部老电影，泡了脚，十点就睡了。一天下来什么特别的事都没做，但我觉得这是最近最充实的一天。也许我们一直在追求「有意义」的生活，却忽略了「没有意义」的日子本身就是一种意义。
text_pinyin: Zhōurì zǎoshang xǐng lái yǐjīng jiǔ diǎn le, chuānglián fèng lǐ tòu jìnlái yì tiáo yángguāng. Wǒ méiyǒu mǎshàng qǐchuáng, tǎng zài bèizi lǐ tīng wàimiàn de shēngyīn. Lóu xià yǒu rén zài liú gǒu, yuǎn chù yǒu xiǎohái zài xiào, ǒu'ěr chuán lái yì shēng niǎo jiào. Jīntiān méiyǒu rènhé ānpái, zhè zhǒng gǎnjué hěn jiǔ méiyǒu guò le. Qǐchuáng yǐhòu wǒ zhǔ le kāfēi, bǎ zǎocān bǎi zài yángtái de xiǎo zhuō shàng, yìbiān chī yìbiān kàn lóu xià de rén lái rén wǎng. Xiàwǔ wǒ fān le yì běn fàng le hěn jiǔ méi kàn wán de shū, kàn le dàgài wǔshí yè. Yǒu shíhou kàn zhe kàn zhe zǒu shén le, jiù fàng xià shū fā yíhuìr dāi, ránhòu zài jiē zhe kàn. Bàngwǎn de shíhou wǒ qù lóu xià sàn le yì quān bù, mǎi le yí gè xīguā huílái. Wǎnshang kàn le yí bù lǎo diànyǐng, pào le jiǎo, shí diǎn jiù shuì le. Yì tiān xiàlái shénme tèbié de shì dōu méi zuò, dàn wǒ juéde zhè shì zuìjìn zuì chōngshí de yì tiān. Yěxǔ wǒmen yìzhí zài zhuīqiú「yǒu yìyì」de shēnghuó, què hūlüè le「méiyǒu yìyì」de rìzi běnshēn jiù shì yì zhǒng yìyì.
text_en: Woke up Sunday morning at nine already, a sliver of sunlight slipping through the curtain gap. I didn't get up right away, just lay under the covers listening to sounds outside. Someone downstairs walking a dog, children laughing in the distance, an occasional bird call. No plans today — a feeling I haven't had in a long time. After getting up I brewed coffee, set breakfast on the small balcony table, eating while watching people come and go below. In the afternoon I picked up a book I'd left unfinished for a while, read about fifty pages. Sometimes my mind wandered and I'd put the book down and zone out, then pick it up again. In the evening I took a lap downstairs, bought a watermelon on the way back. At night I watched an old movie, soaked my feet, and was asleep by ten. Nothing special happened all day, but I felt it was the most fulfilling day in recent memory. Maybe we've always been chasing a 'meaningful' life while overlooking that 'meaningless' days are themselves a kind of meaning.

questions.q_en: What did the author do in the afternoon?

questions.q_zh: 作者对这一天的感受是什么？
questions.q_en: How did the author feel about this day?

options.text: 觉得是最近最充实的一天
options.pinyin: juéde shì zuìjìn zuì chōngshí de yì tiān
options.text_en: Felt it was the most fulfilling day recently

options.pinyin: juéde yīnggāi duō zuò yìxiē shì

title: The Old Jacket
text_zh: 换季的时候整理衣柜，拿出了一件旧外套。它已经洗得有点褪色了，右边的口袋里居然还有一张电影票。日期是四年前的冬天，电影名字我已经不记得了，但我记得那天的事。那是和一个很久没见的老朋友看的。我们高中毕业以后各自去了不同的城市，平时很少联系。那年冬天他来出差，约我出来见个面。我们看完电影以后去了一家小饭馆，点了啤酒和花生米，聊了三个多小时。没有聊什么深刻的话题，就是说说各自的近况，回忆了一些上学时候的事情，笑了很多。分开的时候他说「别太久不联系了」，我说「好」。但后来我们又很久没联系了。我把电影票从口袋里拿出来，夹在书里。旧外套可以扔掉，但这张票我想留着。它不只是一张票，它是一个证据——证明那天晚上我们确实很开心。
text_pinyin: Huàn jì de shíhou zhěnglǐ yīguì, ná chū le yí jiàn jiù wàitào. Tā yǐjīng xǐ de yǒudiǎn tuìsè le, yòubiān de kǒudài lǐ jūrán hái yǒu yì zhāng diànyǐng piào. Rìqī shì sì nián qián de dōngtiān, diànyǐng míngzi wǒ yǐjīng bú jìde le, dàn wǒ jìde nà tiān de shì. Nà shì hé yí gè hěn jiǔ méi jiàn de lǎo péngyou kàn de. Wǒmen gāozhōng bìyè yǐhòu gèzì qù le bùtóng de chéngshì, píngshí hěn shǎo liánxì. Nà nián dōngtiān tā lái chūchāi, yuē wǒ chūlái jiàn gè miàn. Wǒmen kàn wán diànyǐng yǐhòu qù le yì jiā xiǎo fànguǎn, diǎn le píjiǔ hé huāshēng mǐ, liáo le sān gè duō xiǎoshí. Méiyǒu liáo shénme shēnkè de huàtí, jiù shì shuō shuō gèzì de jìnkuàng, huíyì le yìxiē shàngxué shíhou de shìqíng, xiào le hěn duō. Fēnkāi de shíhou tā shuō「bié tài jiǔ bù liánxì le」, wǒ shuō「hǎo」. Dàn hòulái wǒmen yòu hěn jiǔ méi liánxì le. Wǒ bǎ diànyǐng piào cóng kǒudài lǐ ná chūlái, jiā zài shū lǐ. Jiù wàitào kěyǐ rēng diào, dàn zhè zhāng piào wǒ xiǎng liú zhe. Tā bù zhǐ shì yì zhāng piào, tā shì yí gè zhèngjù — zhèngmíng nà tiān wǎnshang wǒmen quèshí hěn kāixīn.
text_en: While organizing the closet for the season change, I pulled out an old jacket. It was already a bit faded from washing, and in the right pocket I found a movie ticket. The date was winter four years ago. I can't remember the movie's name, but I remember that day. I watched it with an old friend I hadn't seen in ages. After graduating high school we went to different cities and rarely kept in touch. That winter he came on a business trip and asked to meet up. After the movie we went to a small restaurant, ordered beer and peanuts, and talked for over three hours. Nothing profound — just catching up on each other's lives, reminiscing about school days, laughing a lot. When parting he said 'don't go so long without being in touch,' and I said 'okay.' But then we went a long time without contact again. I took the ticket out of the pocket and pressed it in a book. The old jacket can be thrown away, but I want to keep this ticket. It's not just a ticket — it's evidence that we really were happy that night.

questions.q_en: What was in the old jacket's pocket?

questions.q_zh: 看完电影以后他们做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did they do after the movie?

options.pinyin: yòu kàn le yì chǎng diànyǐng

options.text: 去小饭馆喝酒聊了三个多小时
options.pinyin: qù xiǎo fànguǎn hē jiǔ liáo le sān gè duō xiǎoshí
options.text_en: Went to a small restaurant and chatted for over three hours

title: The Bus at Dusk
text_zh: 每天傍晚坐公交车回家的时候，阳光刚好从车窗的一侧照进来。如果你坐在靠窗的位置，阳光会把你的脸晒得暖暖的。车里的人大多在看手机，但我喜欢看窗外。这个时间的天空特别好看，是那种橘红色渐渐变成紫色的颜色。有时候经过一座桥，能看到河面上反射着金色的光。我注意到每天差不多同一站上来的几个人：一个穿灰色外套的中年男人，总是靠着门站着闭眼休息；一个年轻女孩，耳朵里塞着耳机，嘴巴无声地跟着歌词在动；一个背着大书包的学生，上车就开始写作业。我不知道他们的名字，不知道他们的故事，但每天看到他们，会有一种奇怪的安心感。好像只要这些人还在，生活就是正常的，世界就是稳定的。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān bàngwǎn zuò gōngjiāo chē huí jiā de shíhou, yángguāng gānghǎo cóng chē chuāng de yí cè zhào jìnlái. Rúguǒ nǐ zuò zài kào chuāng de wèizhì, yángguāng huì bǎ nǐ de liǎn shài de nuǎn nuǎn de. Chē lǐ de rén dàduō zài kàn shǒujī, dàn wǒ xǐhuan kàn chuāng wài. Zhège shíjiān de tiānkōng tèbié hǎokàn, shì nà zhǒng jú hóngsè jiànjiàn biàn chéng zǐsè de yánsè. Yǒu shíhou jīngguò yí zuò qiáo, néng kàn dào hé miàn shàng fǎnshè zhe jīnsè de guāng. Wǒ zhùyì dào měi tiān chàbuduō tóng yí zhàn shàng lái de jǐ gè rén: yí gè chuān huīsè wàitào de zhōngnián nánrén, zǒng shì kào zhe mén zhàn zhe bì yǎn xiūxi; yí gè niánqīng nǚhái, ěrduo lǐ sāi zhe ěrjī, zuǐba wú shēng de gēn zhe gēcí zài dòng; yí gè bēi zhe dà shūbāo de xuéshēng, shàng chē jiù kāishǐ xiě zuòyè. Wǒ bù zhīdào tāmen de míngzi, bù zhīdào tāmen de gùshi, dàn měi tiān kàn dào tāmen, huì yǒu yì zhǒng qíguài de ānxīn gǎn. Hǎoxiàng zhǐyào zhèxiē rén hái zài, shēnghuó jiù shì zhèngcháng de, shìjiè jiù shì wěndìng de.
text_en: Every evening riding the bus home, sunlight shines through the windows from one side. If you sit by the window, the sun warms your face. Most people on the bus look at their phones, but I like looking outside. The sky at this hour is especially beautiful — that kind of orange-red gradually turning to purple. Sometimes crossing a bridge, you can see golden light reflected on the river. I've noticed a few people who board at roughly the same stop every day: a middle-aged man in a gray jacket who always stands by the door with eyes closed, resting; a young girl with earbuds in, lips silently mouthing song lyrics; a student with a big backpack who starts doing homework the moment they board. I don't know their names, don't know their stories, but seeing them every day brings a strange sense of reassurance. As if as long as these people are still here, life is normal and the world is stable.

questions.q_zh: 那个年轻女孩在车上做什么？
questions.q_en: What does the young girl do on the bus?

options.text: 戴着耳机无声地跟着歌词动嘴巴
options.pinyin: dài zhe ěrjī wú shēng de gēn zhe gēcí dòng zuǐba
options.text_en: Wears earbuds and silently mouths song lyrics

questions.q_zh: 每天看到同样的人，作者有什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How does the author feel seeing the same people every day?

options.pinyin: yǒu yì zhǒng qíguài de ānxīn gǎn

title: The Taste of Homesickness
text_zh: 来这个城市工作已经五年了，大部分时间我都不觉得想家。但有时候走在路上，突然闻到一股很熟悉的味道——也许是某家店在炖排骨汤，也许是巷子里飘出来的葱油饼的香味——我的鼻子就会比脑子先反应过来，心里一下子就软了。妈妈做的排骨汤是用砂锅慢慢炖的，要炖两个小时，汤是白色的，上面浮着一层薄薄的油。她总是先给我盛一碗最浓的。我每次回家都要喝两碗，她就在旁边看着我喝，脸上带着那种「吃得多我就放心了」的表情。上个周末我打电话给妈妈，她问我吃得怎么样，我说还行。其实那天我刚吃了一碗方便面。挂了电话以后我决定周末去超市买排骨，自己试着炖一锅汤。炖了两个小时打开锅盖，味道虽然不一样，但那股热气扑在脸上的感觉，让我觉得离家近了一点点。
text_pinyin: Lái zhège chéngshì gōngzuò yǐjīng wǔ nián le, dà bùfen shíjiān wǒ dōu bù juéde xiǎng jiā. Dàn yǒu shíhou zǒu zài lù shàng, tūrán wén dào yì gǔ hěn shúxī de wèidào — yěxǔ shì mǒu jiā diàn zài dùn páigǔ tāng, yěxǔ shì xiàngzi lǐ piāo chūlái de cōngyóu bǐng de xiāngwèi — wǒ de bízi jiù huì bǐ nǎozi xiān fǎnyìng guòlái, xīnlǐ yíxiàzi jiù ruǎn le. Māma zuò de páigǔ tāng shì yòng shāguō mànmàn dùn de, yào dùn liǎng gè xiǎoshí, tāng shì báisè de, shàngmiàn fú zhe yì céng báo báo de yóu. Tā zǒng shì xiān gěi wǒ chéng yì wǎn zuì nóng de. Wǒ měi cì huí jiā dōu yào hē liǎng wǎn, tā jiù zài pángbiān kàn zhe wǒ hē, liǎn shàng dài zhe nà zhǒng「chī de duō wǒ jiù fàngxīn le」de biǎoqíng. Shàng gè zhōumò wǒ dǎ diànhuà gěi māma, tā wèn wǒ chī de zěnmeyàng, wǒ shuō hái xíng. Qíshí nà tiān wǒ gāng chī le yì wǎn fāngbiàn miàn. Guà le diànhuà yǐhòu wǒ juédìng zhōumò qù chāoshì mǎi páigǔ, zìjǐ shì zhe dùn yì guō tāng. Dùn le liǎng gè xiǎoshí dǎkāi guō gài, wèidào suīrán bù yíyàng, dàn nà gǔ rè qì pū zài liǎn shàng de gǎnjué, ràng wǒ juéde lí jiā jìn le yìdiǎndiǎn.
text_en: I've been working in this city for five years, and most of the time I don't feel homesick. But sometimes walking down the street, I suddenly catch a familiar smell — maybe a shop stewing pork rib soup, maybe the aroma of scallion pancakes drifting from an alley — and my nose reacts before my brain does, my heart going soft all at once. Mom's pork rib soup was slowly stewed in a clay pot for two hours, the broth white with a thin layer of oil floating on top. She'd always serve me the richest bowl first. Every time I went home I'd drink two bowls while she watched, wearing that 'as long as you eat a lot I'm reassured' expression. Last weekend I called mom and she asked how I was eating. I said fine. Actually I'd just eaten instant noodles. After hanging up I decided to go to the supermarket that weekend and buy ribs to try stewing a pot of soup myself. After two hours I lifted the lid — the taste wasn't quite the same, but the hot steam hitting my face made me feel just a little closer to home.

questions.q_zh: 什么会让作者突然想家？
questions.q_en: What makes the author suddenly homesick?

questions.q_zh: 作者自己炖的汤和妈妈的一样吗？
questions.q_en: Was the author's soup the same as mom's?

options.text: 味道不一样，但热气的感觉让人觉得离家近了
options.pinyin: wèidào bù yíyàng, dàn rè qì de gǎnjué ràng rén juéde lí jiā jìn le
options.text_en: Taste was different, but the steam made it feel closer to home

options.pinyin: bǐ māma zuò de hái hǎo hē

title: The Slow Season
text_zh: 秋天是一个慢下来的季节。树叶用很长的时间变颜色，从绿色慢慢变成黄色，再变成红色，最后安静地落在地上。不像春天那样急着发芽，也不像夏天那样热烈地生长。秋天好像在告诉我们：结束也可以是美丽的。我最喜欢秋天傍晚的光线，又柔又暖，把所有东西都染上了一层金色。走在银杏树下面，脚踩着厚厚的落叶，发出沙沙的声音。空气里有一种说不出来的香味，是落叶的味道，是果实成熟的味道，也是时间流过的味道。每年秋天我都会给自己放一天假，什么工作也不想，就去公园坐一个下午。看叶子落下来，看老人打太极，看小孩追着落叶跑。这些画面每年都一样，但每年看的时候心情都不太一样。也许这就是季节存在的意义——用同样的风景提醒你，你已经不是去年的你了。
text_pinyin: Qiūtiān shì yí gè màn xiàlái de jìjié. Shùyè yòng hěn cháng de shíjiān biàn yánsè, cóng lǜsè mànmàn biàn chéng huángsè, zài biàn chéng hóngsè, zuìhòu ānjìng de luò zài dì shàng. Bú xiàng chūntiān nàyàng jí zhe fāyá, yě bú xiàng xiàtiān nàyàng rèliè de shēngzhǎng. Qiūtiān hǎoxiàng zài gàosu wǒmen: jiéshù yě kěyǐ shì měilì de. Wǒ zuì xǐhuan qiūtiān bàngwǎn de guāngxiàn, yòu róu yòu nuǎn, bǎ suǒyǒu dōngxi dōu rǎn shàng le yì céng jīnsè. Zǒu zài yínxìng shù xiàmiàn, jiǎo cǎi zhe hòu hòu de luòyè, fāchū shāshā de shēngyīn. Kōngqì lǐ yǒu yì zhǒng shuō bu chūlái de xiāngwèi, shì luòyè de wèidào, shì guǒshí chéngshú de wèidào, yě shì shíjiān liú guò de wèidào. Měi nián qiūtiān wǒ dōu huì gěi zìjǐ fàng yì tiān jià, shénme gōngzuò yě bù xiǎng, jiù qù gōngyuán zuò yí gè xiàwǔ. Kàn yèzi luò xiàlái, kàn lǎorén dǎ tàijí, kàn xiǎohái zhuī zhe luòyè pǎo. Zhèxiē huàmiàn měi nián dōu yíyàng, dàn měi nián kàn de shíhou xīnqíng dōu bú tài yíyàng. Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì jìjié cúnzài de yìyì — yòng tóngyàng de fēngjǐng tíxǐng nǐ, nǐ yǐjīng bú shì qùnián de nǐ le.
text_en: Autumn is a season that slows down. Leaves take a long time to change color, gradually from green to yellow, then to red, finally falling quietly to the ground. Not rushing to sprout like spring, nor growing fiercely like summer. Autumn seems to tell us: endings can be beautiful too. I love autumn's evening light best — soft and warm, coating everything in a layer of gold. Walking beneath ginkgo trees, feet crunching through thick fallen leaves with a rustling sound. The air carries an indescribable fragrance — the scent of fallen leaves, of ripe fruit, of time passing. Every autumn I give myself a day off, think nothing of work, and just sit in a park for an afternoon. Watch leaves fall, watch elders practice tai chi, watch children chase fallen leaves. These scenes are the same every year, but my mood while watching is always a little different. Perhaps that's the meaning of seasons — using the same scenery to remind you that you're no longer who you were last year.

questions.q_zh: 作者觉得秋天好像在告诉我们什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author think autumn seems to tell us?

options.pinyin: jiéshù yě kěyǐ shì měilì de

questions.q_zh: 作者认为季节存在的意义是什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author think is the meaning of seasons?

options.pinyin: ràng rén xiǎngshòu bùtóng de tiānqì

options.text: 用同样的风景提醒你已经变了
options.pinyin: yòng tóngyàng de fēngjǐng tíxǐng nǐ yǐjīng biàn le
options.text_en: Using same scenery to remind you that you've changed

options.pinyin: gěi rén fàng jià de jīhuì
options.text_en: To give people chances for vacation

title: The Repairman
text_zh: 小区门口有一个修鞋修包的摊位，是一位六十多岁的老师傅开的。他的摊位很小，只有一张桌子、一把椅子和一个装满工具的箱子。我的一双皮鞋开了胶，本来想扔掉买新的，但路过他的摊位的时候就停下来了。老师傅接过鞋看了看，说「能修，二十块钱」。他戴上老花镜，用一把小刷子仔细地把旧胶清理干净，涂上新胶，用夹子夹好，放在一边晾干。整个过程大概十五分钟，他做得很慢，但每一步都很仔细。等的时候我和他聊了几句，他说在这里摆了快二十年了。以前来修的人很多，现在越来越少了，大家都习惯坏了就买新的。他说「现在的东西也不如以前耐用了，修的意义确实没那么大了」。拿到修好的鞋以后，我觉得这双鞋好像比新鞋还让我珍惜。因为有人愿意花时间把它修好，而不是简单地把它扔掉。
text_pinyin: Xiǎoqū ménkǒu yǒu yí gè xiū xié xiū bāo de tānwèi, shì yí wèi liùshí duō suì de lǎo shīfu kāi de. Tā de tānwèi hěn xiǎo, zhǐ yǒu yì zhāng zhuōzi, yì bǎ yǐzi hé yí gè zhuāng mǎn gōngjù de xiāngzi. Wǒ de yì shuāng píxié kāi le jiāo, běnlái xiǎng rēng diào mǎi xīn de, dàn lùguò tā de tānwèi de shíhou jiù tíng xiàlái le. Lǎo shīfu jiē guò xié kàn le kàn, shuō「néng xiū, èrshí kuài qián」. Tā dài shàng lǎohuā jìng, yòng yì bǎ xiǎo shuāzi zǐxì de bǎ jiù jiāo qīnglǐ gānjìng, tú shàng xīn jiāo, yòng jiāzi jiā hǎo, fàng zài yìbiān liàng gān. Zhěnggè guòchéng dàgài shíwǔ fēnzhōng, tā zuò de hěn màn, dàn měi yí bù dōu hěn zǐxì. Děng de shíhou wǒ hé tā liáo le jǐ jù, tā shuō zài zhèlǐ bǎi le kuài èrshí nián le. Yǐqián lái xiū de rén hěn duō, xiànzài yuè lái yuè shǎo le, dàjiā dōu xíguàn huài le jiù mǎi xīn de. Tā shuō「xiànzài de dōngxi yě bù rú yǐqián nàiyòng le, xiū de yìyì quèshí méi nàme dà le」. Ná dào xiū hǎo de xié yǐhòu, wǒ juéde zhè shuāng xié hǎoxiàng bǐ xīn xié hái ràng wǒ zhēnxī. Yīnwèi yǒu rén yuànyì huā shíjiān bǎ tā xiū hǎo, ér bú shì jiǎndān de bǎ tā rēng diào.
text_en: Near the compound entrance there's a shoe and bag repair stall run by a craftsman in his sixties. His stall is small — just a table, a chair, and a toolbox. My leather shoes had come unglued and I was going to throw them out and buy new ones, but I stopped when passing his stall. The old master took the shoes, looked them over, and said 'fixable, twenty yuan.' He put on reading glasses, carefully cleaned off the old glue with a small brush, applied new glue, clamped them, and set them aside to dry. The whole process took about fifteen minutes — he worked slowly, but every step was careful. While waiting I chatted with him. He said he'd been here nearly twenty years. There used to be many customers; now fewer and fewer, since everyone is used to buying new when things break. He said 'things nowadays aren't as durable as before, so repairing really doesn't matter as much anymore.' After getting the repaired shoes back, I felt I treasured them more than new ones. Because someone was willing to spend time fixing them, rather than simply throwing them away.

questions.q_en: How long did the shoe repair take?

questions.q_zh: 老师傅为什么说来修东西的人越来越少了？
questions.q_en: Why does the old master say fewer people come for repairs?

options.text: 大家习惯坏了就买新的
options.pinyin: dàjiā xíguàn huài le jiù mǎi xīn de
options.text_en: Everyone is used to buying new when things break
The stall is too far away

title: Three in the Morning
text_zh: 凌晨三点醒来，发现窗外很安静。没有车的声音，没有人说话的声音，连虫子都不叫了。这种安静不是白天那种被噪音包围然后突然安静的感觉，而是一种真正的、完整的安静。我没有再去睡，而是起来倒了一杯水，坐在客厅的沙发上。黑暗中可以看到各种东西模糊的轮廓：茶几上的杯子，墙上的挂钟，地上那双拖鞋。白天的时候这些东西太普通了，不会注意到它们。但在凌晨三点，它们好像都变得安静而郑重，像是在认真地待在自己的位置上。我想起了一句话：「深夜是属于诚实的人的」。也许是因为在这个时间，所有的面具都摘下来了，所有不必要的想法都沉淀了，剩下的只有最真实的自己。喝完那杯水，我觉得心里平静了很多。然后回去躺下，很快就睡着了。
text_pinyin: Língchén sān diǎn xǐng lái, fāxiàn chuāng wài hěn ānjìng. Méiyǒu chē de shēngyīn, méiyǒu rén shuōhuà de shēngyīn, lián chóngzi dōu bú jiào le. Zhè zhǒng ānjìng bú shì báitiān nà zhǒng bèi zàoyīn bāowéi ránhòu tūrán ānjìng de gǎnjué, ér shì yì zhǒng zhēnzhèng de, wánzhěng de ānjìng. Wǒ méiyǒu zài qù shuì, ér shì qǐlái dào le yì bēi shuǐ, zuò zài kètīng de shāfā shàng. Hēi'àn zhōng kěyǐ kàn dào gè zhǒng dōngxi móhu de lúnkuò: chájī shàng de bēizi, qiáng shàng de guà zhōng, dì shàng nà shuāng tuōxié. Báitiān de shíhou zhèxiē dōngxi tài pǔtōng le, bú huì zhùyì dào tāmen. Dàn zài língchén sān diǎn, tāmen hǎoxiàng dōu biàn de ānjìng ér zhèngzhòng, xiàng shì zài rènzhēn de dāi zài zìjǐ de wèizhì shàng. Wǒ xiǎngqǐ le yí jù huà:「shēnyè shì shǔyú chéngshí de rén de」. Yěxǔ shì yīnwèi zài zhège shíjiān, suǒyǒu de miànjù dōu zhāi xiàlái le, suǒyǒu bú bìyào de xiǎngfǎ dōu chéndiàn le, shèng xià de zhǐ yǒu zuì zhēnshí de zìjǐ. Hē wán nà bēi shuǐ, wǒ juéde xīnlǐ píngjìng le hěn duō. Ránhòu huíqù tǎng xià, hěn kuài jiù shuì zháo le.
text_en: Woke at three in the morning to find it perfectly quiet outside. No cars, no voices, not even insects. This quiet isn't the daytime kind — noise surrounding you then suddenly stopping — but a true, complete quiet. I didn't go back to sleep. Instead I got up, poured a glass of water, and sat on the living room sofa. In the darkness I could see the vague outlines of things: the cup on the coffee table, the wall clock, the slippers on the floor. During the day these objects are too ordinary to notice. But at three in the morning they all seem quiet and solemn, as if earnestly staying in their places. I remembered a saying: 'The deep night belongs to honest people.' Maybe because at this hour all masks are removed, all unnecessary thoughts have settled, and what remains is only your most authentic self. After finishing that glass of water, my heart felt much calmer. Then I lay back down and fell asleep quickly.

questions.q_zh: 凌晨三点的安静和白天的安静有什么不同？
questions.q_en: How is the quiet at 3 AM different from daytime quiet?

options.text: 是一种真正的、完整的安静
options.pinyin: shì yì zhǒng zhēnzhèng de, wánzhěng de ānjìng

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么觉得深夜属于诚实的人？
questions.q_en: Why does the author feel the deep night belongs to honest people?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi méiyǒu rén kàn dào nǐ

options.text: 因为所有面具都摘下来了，只剩最真实的自己
options.pinyin: yīnwèi suǒyǒu miànjù dōu zhāi xiàlái le, zhǐ shèng zuì zhēnshí de zìjǐ
options.text_en: Because all masks are removed, only the most authentic self remains
Because working at night is more efficient
